L1.area: Markdown parsing extensions and native C bindings

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the parsing of markdown table marker rows. When a table contains more than UINT16_MAX (65535) columns, the column counter wraps around, leading to heap memory corruption. The impact ranges from information disclosure to arbitrary code execution when processing attacker-controlled markdown input.

L3.area: Markdown parsing extensions and native C bindings
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the parsing of markdown table marker rows. When a table contains more than UINT16_MAX (65535) columns, the column counter wraps around, leading to heap memory corruption. The impact ranges from information disclosure to arbitrary code execution when processing attacker-controlled markdown input.

ground_truth.reason: The table row parsing logic increments a uint16_t column counter without checking for overflow past UINT16_MAX, causing an integer wraparound that leads to heap memory corruption when processing tables with more than 65535 columns.
